title: Formula Feedback!
excerpt:
IF({Days to Complete Inquiry}> “1”,“:white_check_mark:”,“”, IF({Days to Complete Inquiry}<=“5”,“:white_check_mark:”, “”)) 
I am trying to use this to checkbox an item when it takes between 2-5 days for a ticket to comple…

posts.author: SkylarMS (Skylar Stillwagon)
posts.cookedHtml:
IF({Days to Complete Inquiry}> “1”,“”,“”, IF({Days to Complete Inquiry}<=“5”,“”, “”))

I am trying to use this to checkbox an item when it takes between 2-5 days for a ticket to complete. I have a 1 day ticket formula and a plus 5 day that are both working. This one is check boxing everything OVER 5 as well. Any tips on how I would get this to represent 2, 3, 4, or 5 days? First time on here, but this will be my new home

posts.author: JCorrell (Jim - The Monday Man)
posts.cookedHtml:
@SkylarMS

Assuming that {Days to Complete Inquiry} is a number column, the comparison values should not be in quotes. There are some other issues also.

Try this:
IF(AND({Days to Complete Inquiry} > 1, {Days to Complete Inquiry} <= 5), ":white_check_mark:", "")

posts.author: SkylarMS (Skylar Stillwagon)
posts.cookedHtml: Thank you @JCorrell, that worked! I have been struggling a few days on that one, so thank you!

posts.author: JCorrell (Jim - The Monday Man)
posts.cookedHtml:
@SkylarMS

You are welcome. And, welcome to the monday-verse!
